extraction.one_line_summary: HODEG won the sprint after Deceuninck-Quick-Step maintained composure through chaotic finale positioning, beating BENNETT who was trapped wide on barriers.
tactical_patterns: Deceuninck-Quick-Step conserved resources by sitting neutral until 1100m while Trek-Segafredo burned energy leading, revealing positioning patience outweighs tempo control.
BENNETT isolated wide on barriers after following Bahrain domestique drift shows sprinter vulnerability when following unfamiliar leadout trains in messy finales.
DÉMARE's FDJ leadout collapsed early forcing lone follow tactics, suggesting depth issues when SINKELDAM went down with 2-3km remaining.

rider_signals.HODEG Álvaro José: positive: delivered first WT win with full team support after team previously prioritized GILBERT overall, showing sprinter capability when unleashed
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: beaten despite red jersey position, trapped wide by poor positioning choices following non-FDJ leadout, showing tactical vulnerability in chaos
rider_signals.HIRSCH Marc: neutral: Sunweb uncertain whether deploying him as sprinter or BAUHAUS, 4 seconds off GC suggests TT capability could threaten WELLENS tomorrow
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: negative: finished fifth after isolated late surge, FDJ leadout dysfunction left him tactically compromised despite being Milan-Sanremo winner
rider_signals.THEUNS Edward: neutral: Trek-Segafredo worked primarily for him over MOSETTI, finished third showing sprint form but squad wasted energy leading finale
rider_signals.SINKELDAM Ramon: negative: crashed helping DÉMARE's leadout, FDJ's key sprint domestique loss exposed team's shallow depth for stage racing
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: holds 4-second GC lead over HIRSCH into 8km TT, margin vulnerable given HIRSCH's age and specialist capability
